mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent labor market expansion, which supports household income growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that generates wage inflation fears and increases the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate a synchronized expansion across goods production and labor markets, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically when the federal funds rate is below 1%, because low financing costs amplify the housing demand signal's transmission to broad economic activity and equity earnings.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the end of zero-interest-rate policy, increasing short-term borrowing costs and discount rates, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals contracting aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly declines in GDPC1 are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation raise real discount rates and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL do not correspond to negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer spending, which accounts for the majority of aggregate demand and quickly translates into lower revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
